claim: The existing todos.jsonl is already a proto-R&D tracker — ~15 of 19 active todos informally encode R&D stage in the free-text progress field.

Two orthogonal state dimensions are required: status (execution: pending/in-progress/blocked/completed/archived) and stage (R&D lifecycle: IDEA through COMPLETE). These are independent axes and must NOT be conflated into a single combined state.
A type discriminator (type: PROJECT|TASK) is mandatory to prevent stage overhead on simple task items. TASK-type items have no stage field or stage lifecycle.

Stage transitions should use a guarded whitelist table (not a strict DAG or strict linear sequence). Unspecified transitions are forbidden. Shortcuts and backtracking are encoded as explicit allowed transitions.

The minimal transition log schema requires: from, to, at (ISO-8601), by (user|auto-advance|migration), optional skipped[] array, optional reason string. Entries are append-only, embedded as stage_history[] in the todo record.

The allowed transition whitelist is: IDEA→{BRAINSTORM,RESEARCH,PLAN,IMPLEMENT,ABANDONED}; BRAINSTORM→{RESEARCH,PLAN,IDEA,ABANDONED}; RESEARCH→{PLAN,BRAINSTORM,IDEA,ABANDONED}; PLAN→{IMPLEMENT,RESEARCH,BRAINSTORM,ABANDONED}; IMPLEMENT→{TEST,PLAN,ABANDONED}; TEST→{COMPLETE,IMPLEMENT,PLAN,ABANDONED}; COMPLETE→{} (terminal); ABANDONED→{} (terminal).
The transition table should be stored in a separate config file (e.g., .planning/rnd-config.json), not embedded in skill logic. This is the declarative_over_imperative pattern (tenet T17).
Skipped stages must be explicitly recorded in a skipped[] array in the transition log entry at transition time — do not rely on post-hoc inference from absent stage_history entries.
Backtracking can be approximated with a returnAfter field on the current todo record pointing to the intended resumption stage. Clear this field when the backtrack is resolved.

The deep-research skill pipeline (Phases 0-5 with decimal sub-phases 2.5, 3.5, 3.95, etc.) is the strongest internal reference for multi-phase lifecycle state machine design — it demonstrates sequential phases, conditional execution, off-ramps, backtracking, and crash-proof state persistence without a formal FSM runtime.

Terminal states require COMPLETE (successful outcome) and ABANDONED (explicit cancellation before reaching COMPLETE). PARKED is an optional soft-pause state with outgoing transitions back to any prior stage (history-state semantics).
Stage state is stored in the todo record itself (embedded stage_history[] array) — not in a separate state file. The todo record IS the state file. This satisfies tenet T9 (crash-proof state).
Claude Code has a native FileChanged hook event that fires when a watched file changes. SessionStart hook can output a watchPaths array to register specific file paths for monitoring.

The correct primary auto-advance mode is lazy on-demand scanning at /rnd view time. Scan all 4 canonical locations per active slug with fs.existsSync() + content checks. Takes <5ms for 20 slugs. Zero overhead between sessions.
FileChanged hook is the correct secondary path for in-session real-time feedback. It complements lazy scan: lazy scan catches between-session advances; FileChanged catches in-session advances.

L3 content check is the correct 'done enough' threshold: BRAINSTORM.md (size>200b AND contains('## ')), RESEARCH_OUTPUT.md (size>1000b AND contains('## ')), FINDINGS.md (size>500b), metadata.json (valid JSON with status field).

Node.js fs.watch on Windows produces duplicate events, null filenames, memory leaks, and junction inconsistencies. Debouncing (100ms window + 1000ms ceiling) is mandatory for all watcher implementations.
Windows path normalization (filePath.replace(/\\/g, '/')) must happen at every hook boundary before regex matching. The existing pattern in post-write-validator.js:117 is the canonical reference.

FileChanged hook matcher matches by basename only. Multiple R&D slugs with BRAINSTORM.md or RESEARCH_OUTPUT.md will all trigger the same hook. Path-based disambiguation must happen inside the hook script using the file_path field.
Session-start hook is the correct registration point for watchPaths — not pre/post-commit hooks. Session-start already reads todos.jsonl and has the data needed to enumerate active R&D slugs.
Atomic writes for state files must use temp-file-then-rename pattern. The session-start.js already implements this at lines 132-153. The rnd-stage-advance hook should use the same pattern.

research-index.jsonl has inconsistent status fields — some entries have status: 'complete', others have no status field. The L5 trigger approach (research-index entry as completion signal) requires enforcement before it can be relied on.
Polling (cron/setTimeout loop) is explicitly rejected for stage advance. Unnecessary overhead for solo-dev scale where lazy scan is instantaneous.
No cross-project findings reference field exists in any SoNash JSONL today. The findings_refs pattern is a genuine design gap confirmed by direct codebase inspection.

research-index.jsonl operates at topic level only (keyword-based discovery of research topics). findings_refs must operate at claim level — topic-level granularity is insufficient for cross-project knowledge flow.

The minimum viable findings_refs data shape: array of objects with ref (<slug>:<claim-id>), relationship (informedBy|constrainedBy|contradicts), and note (human-readable rationale). No new infrastructure required — plain JSONL extension.

Stable claim addressing requires <topic-slug>:<claim-id> format. The slug is stable once research is archived (archive pattern makes slugs immutable). Old C-NNN IDs are stable as long as claims.jsonl is never regenerated after archiving.

Over 95% of post-retraction citations are made in error. When a claim's confidence degrades to LOW or REFUTED status is applied, all todos referencing that claim via findings_refs must surface a warning requiring acknowledgment.

Pull mechanism (session-begin scan of active todos with findings_refs, acknowledgment-required digest) is preferred over push for solo developers. Push creates notification fatigue; pull scopes to active work only.

Triple stores (RDF), property graph databases (Neo4j, ArangoDB), and embedding-based auto-retrieval are all explicitly rejected at SoNash's scale (~800 claims, ~20 todos). JSONL with jq lookup is sufficient.

Auto-population of findings_refs via embedding similarity is an anti-pattern: false positive rate is unacceptable at solo-dev context density. Manual registration with required note rationale is the correct pattern.
The existing routing field in claims.jsonl is the push-side complement to findings_refs. Extending routing with a projects[] array (project-slug routing) enables push-side cross-project surfacing.
Obsidian block references (^block-id syntax) are proprietary and break outside Obsidian. They are not a viable cross-reference mechanism for SoNash's file-based JSONL approach.
The existing todos.jsonl already has a de facto PROJECT/TASK split: ~15 items are multi-session R&D projects, ~5 are simple atomic tasks. The type field formalizes existing behavior.
Type selection must never block item capture. Default type is 'task'. AI infers type from content signals (advisory only). Promotion from TASK to PROJECT is a subsequent explicit action.

NN/G's progressive disclosure research establishes a 2-layer maximum for usability. Layer 1: standard menu for all items. Layer 2: PROJECT-specific actions surface only when a PROJECT item is selected. No third layer.

The Progress column should be replaced by a Stage/Note column that renders [STAGE] badge for PROJECT items and free-text for TASK items. No new columns needed. Backward-compatible with hook parsers that parse by column name.

/rnd should be a thin view-layer on the same todos.jsonl filtered to type: 'project' items, not a separate storage backend. Both /todo and /rnd share the same JSONL and render script — no sync state required.

The minimum new metadata for PROJECT items is exactly two fields: type: 'project' (discriminator) and stage: string (one of 7 pipeline values). All other metadata remains in existing description and progress fields.
The /todo header must show 'N projects · M tasks active' to provide minimum discoverability of the type split. Hiding project management behind secondary menus is the Todoist anti-pattern.
Type auto-detection from content signals (multi-sentence descriptions, keywords: research/brainstorm/phase/plan suggest PROJECT) is viable but must remain advisory. Never auto-promote silently.
The current todos.jsonl has NO schema_version field in any of its 19 records. All records are implicitly schema version 1.
SoNash already uses per-record schema_version integer in production JSONL via the BaseRecord pattern in scripts/reviews/lib/schemas/shared.ts. todos.jsonl migration must follow this same standard.

All proposed new todos.jsonl fields are additive (no removals or renames). render-todos.js uses ?? fallbacks on all field accesses — already forward-compatible. Existing consumers require no code changes.

Zod v4's .default() applies even when a key is entirely absent from the input (changed from Zod v3 behavior). V1 records parsed through V2 schema automatically receive type: 'task' and schema_version: 2 defaults.

The bulk migration script pattern (migrate-todos-v2.js) follows the exact pattern of migrate-retros.js and migrate-ecosystem-v2.js: backup via git commit → dry-run → apply full-file overwrite → validate with render-todos.js. Idempotent: if schema_version >= 2 skip.
Rollback for the migration is git checkout -- .planning/todos.jsonl. No separate backup mechanism needed for a Git-tracked 20-record file.
The Verzod library has a Zod v3 peer dependency incompatible with SoNash's Zod v4. It must not be added as a dependency. The migration pattern is implementable in ~50 lines of plain TypeScript.

The initial stage_history migration entry must use by: 'migration' with reason: 'retroactive_v2_migration' to distinguish inferred stages from user-confirmed transitions. Follows migrate-retros.js precedent (origin: {type: 'migration'}).

The two-schema approach (ReadSchema lenient + WriteSchema strict) matches the existing review pipeline pattern. ReadSchema uses .optional() with defaults for all new fields. WriteSchema requires schema_version: 2 and type enum.

The Planning tab's existing ResearchTopics widget shows historical topic summaries only. It does NOT show current pipeline stage, blocked projects, time-in-stage, or dependency relationships. The structural gap between research history and pipeline state is confirmed.

A 7th R&D tab should be added to the dev dashboard rather than folding R&D into the Planning tab. The decisive argument: distinct user intent (sprint readiness vs pipeline health are different cognitive modes).

The R&D pipeline data builder (build-rnd.js) requires a 3-source join: todos.jsonl (primary, project identity/stage), .research/<slug>/metadata.json (research completion status), and .planning/<slug>/ directory (plan existence, decisions count).

7 tabs exceeds the 3-6 tab consumer UI guideline by 1. This is non-blocking for an internal developer tool — Grafana, DataDog, GitHub all routinely exceed 8 tabs. Expert users internalize layouts quickly.

High-value cross-tab links (3 only): R&D→Planning (project hits IMPLEMENT, surface sprint tasks), R&D→Debt (project has associated MASTER_DEBT entries), Debt→R&D (debt item has blocking project). All other cross-tab links (Reviews, Pipeline hooks, Audits) are low-value noise.
The R&D tab should be built last (after all 6 existing tabs). This provides a timing advantage: the todos.jsonl type/stage schema extensions will exist before the R&D tab needs them.

The primary R&D visualization is a stage-column kanban (projects as cards, pipeline stages as columns). Secondary: timeline table showing entry-date per stage per project for stall identification. No current tab uses this visualization type.

Theoretical saturation (grounded theory) is the correct conceptual frame for scouting termination. Saturation is a matter of degree (diminishing returns), not a discrete event. Stop when marginal insight per input has declined to negligible.

The ≤5% new-information threshold (Hennig et al., PLOS ONE 2020) provides a concrete saturation test: when (new concepts in last N sources) / (total concepts so far) falls below 5%, saturation is reached. At 0% for two consecutive sources from different sub-categories, it is definitive.

Chesterton's Fence is the mandatory pre-adopt/reject gate: do not adopt OR reject a pattern until you can articulate (a) the problem it solves, (b) the context constraints that shaped it, (c) what happens without those constraints. This is the minimum rigor that prevents cargo-cult adoption.

Cargo cult adoption is identifiable by: justification by tradition/authority without reasoning, ignoring tradeoffs, resistance to inspection, and mimicking artifacts without causes. The detection test: can you name (a) the problem, (b) context assumptions, (c) what happens without those assumptions?

NIH syndrome and Cargo Cult are the two failure poles. The bias-neutral evaluation: 'Does this pattern solve a real problem we have, and does it solve it in a way that fits our constraints?' This avoids both 'adopt because others do' and 'reject because we didn't invent it'.

Rogers' Five Factors provide the adopt/adapt/reject decision backbone: Relative Advantage, Compatibility, Complexity, Trialability, Observability. ADOPT when all favorable; ADAPT when advantage is clear but compatibility requires modification; REJECT when advantage is unclear or context requirements unmet.

Herbert Simon's satisficing framework resolves 'no artificial caps but not infinite': stop when the aspiration level (enough to decide) is met, not when a counter expires. The aspiration level is qualitative and researcher-defined.

ADR (Architecture Decision Record) lifecycle — Draft → Proposed → Accepted/Rejected — is the correct exit artifact pattern for scouting. Scouting is 'done' when a SCOUT-DECISION document exists for each category with: pattern, context requirements, adopt/adapt/reject, and translation adjustments.

The AAER sustainability test ('If you removed the organizational scaffolding this pattern was designed for, would it still deliver value?') filters against patterns whose value is entirely context-dependent in ways solo devs cannot replicate.

Rabbit hole detection signals: you feel busy but cannot name a concrete deliverable, scope keeps expanding, you are avoiding synthesis/writing, you keep switching approaches without justification. The tangible output test: 'What artifact will this produce?'

The convergence-loop skill has an explicit Programmatic Mode designed for integration by other skills. The minimum interface: claims_source + preset + topic → convergence_status + confidence + cl_state_ref + corrections_applied.

CL-PROTOCOL (policy: which checks to run, when, which agents) and /convergence-loop (execution: T20 tally machinery) are intentionally separate and must remain so. Decision D5 independence must be respected.

Three existing skills already integrate CL with different obligation levels: /deep-plan (MUST for L/XL 5+ claims, SHOULD for S/M), /deep-research (MUST quick-pass + Phase 3 research-claims preset), /brainstorm (SHOULD at Phase 0 3+ claims and Phase 4). These are the pattern precedents.

CRITICAL CROSS-CUTTING: Skill-completed stages are already pre-verified. The /rnd pipeline must NOT add redundant CL gates when deep-research, deep-plan, or brainstorm were used — those skills already ran CL internally. The orchestrator must distinguish skill-generated artifacts from manually-created artifacts.

Artifact detection for skill-completion: claims.jsonl alone is insufficient (user may have aborted before Phase 2.5). Require claims.jsonl AND at least one V*.md verifier file in findings/ directory. If V*.md absent, treat as manually-created.

The embedded CL model (each stage runs its own CL before advancing) is wrong: (1) early stages have <3 claims (below CL minimum), (2) existing skills already embed CL (redundant), (3) cannot be skipped without modifying stage logic.

The separate invocation model (fully manual CL) is rejected by existing D11 decision ('Verifications at ALL tiers T0-T3'). The CL-PROTOCOL origin note documents exactly this failure from Session #237: CLs were forgotten, single-pass agents run instead.

CL obligation by stage transition: IDEA→BRAINSTORM (MAY/skip, <3 claims); BRAINSTORM→RESEARCH (SHOULD, quick preset); RESEARCH→PLAN (MUST if manual / satisfied-by-skill if deep-research used); PLAN→IMPLEMENT (MUST if manual / satisfied-by-skill if deep-plan used); IMPLEMENT→TEST (MAY); TEST→COMPLETE (SHOULD, quick).

CL output must NOT trigger auto-advance. CL always requires a user gate (SKILL.md Rule 3: user decides convergence). The pipeline state records the CL result (cl_verified: true/partial/false/skipped) but stage advancement requires separate user approval.

CL results should be stored in a cl_transitions[] audit array in the /rnd state file, linking the CL run to the stage transition. Each entry: from_stage, to_stage, cl_state_ref, convergence_status, timestamp.
